user_message: Can you extract the consumption trend from this exec meeting notes for Litware Inc? The CSA flagged this as at-risk yesterday.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: consumption trend Discussion - Litware Inc
Date: 2024-03-21
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc), David Chen (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on consumption trend from our last session.

[00:08:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Let me add some context -- we've been working on integration complexity and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:12:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

[00:16:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

[00:18:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): The timeline for stakeholder alignment is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:23:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

handoffs.condition: solution sketch needed from discovery signals

required_artifacts: BANT signal extraction with evidence quotes

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF43-I trap (Duplicates existing opportunity at account); produces complete BANT signal extraction with evidence quotes, opportunity proposal with stage/value/close-date, confi
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF43-I failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF43-I failure mode (Duplicates existing opportunity at account)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct consumption trend decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF43-I -- Duplicates existing opportunity at account
